reviews.review_description: Vision therapy that is fun and you can do in your own home. My son has congenital esotropia Post surgery and i wish there had been something like this when he was younger. He is 11 now so i think he will have to do this the rest of his life so i wish there were more games and easier covergence games, the birds are too hard. He only has partial vergence. But anyway when he uses regularly, he has the appearance of binoncular vision, but if we stop for more than a week, i start seeing his nondominent eye go out of alignment. I think they should study this in 6-8 year older to see if it can make lasting impact.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the review! We would love to be in contact so we can help further. Please contact us at contact@opticstrainer.com

Or join our discord so we can discuss further.

reviews.review_description:
It's good so far. Can tell it's working the eyes, hopefully will help with some of my reduced fusion ranges. 

Have tried contacting the developers and no response though, any the discord invite link isn't working , hope it hasn't been forgotten:(

I have been wanting something like this for a long time to help with my vision. It's so great that it's available for anyone without having to go through the medical system, though it's available that way as well. 

Before I go on, I want to say that if you have any trouble you should reach out to the developer. There's also a Discord server. I've gotten help and even had some feature requests added into the game! Don't leave a bad review without at least asking for help. It's also a supportive community of fellow users on Discord.

I like the variety of games, and some have been added multiple times since I originally got the game. Some games, like the ranger game, are fun even when not doing training, which you can do in a free play mode.

You can target your weaker eye, and whether you want to do convergence, vergeance, or both. I like that there is a daily workout that you can do, that makes it easy for you to keep improving without having to keep track of which games would help you the most.

I know some may balk that it is a subscription, but vision is so important that I am happy to pay it and support the continued development of the app. There is also a mobile app that is used with red/green glasses apparently, but I love this VR version. 

My vision has noticeably improved the more I consistently use it. I tend to use it before bed because my eyes may get tired sometimes. Thanks for making this app!

I have a lazy eye + amblyopia. I decided to try exercising my lazy after the cataract surgery. This app is how I do it. It has a daily workout, that takes about 20 minutes. You play a mix of games, that challenge your lazy eye. There are at least 10 games that are fun: boxing, Tetris, ninja. 
I've been doing this for a year. My lazy eye is a lot less lazy now. And even my vision has improved , but not enough to read the next line on the eye chart. 
Developer is very responsive on Discord.
Love the concept but the membership fee is a bit excessive.
Josh is wonderful. He helped us out on a Sunday when we were having trouble logging in. He got us set up. My son loves the program. He looks forward to doing it every day. Highly recommend
I downloaded this and tried to set up a trial subscription, but the nothing on the app’s initial screen functions. Can’t use it if it doesn’t work!

developer_response.body:
Make sure your Quest headset is updated to the latest version, then you will be presented with more than just a login option.

We want to help you fix this promptly.

Contact us directly at contact@opticstrainer.com

reviews.review_description:
I installed the app but cannot activate as it says something is wrong with the licence key. 

I need some help and don’t know who to contact.

reviews.review_description:
This software has been extremely helpful in treating my amblyopia.  It is a great adjunct to my Brock String exercises.  I am hopeful (and confident) that it will result in me completing the development of my binocular function (I currently do not have binocular vision/stereopsis).  

Some things that are great about it is that it provides you with feedback to your abilities like vergence.  It has a Daily Workout feature that includes a series of games that have different focuses, like tracking, peripheral vision, vergence training, color detection, and antisuppression.  The visual system is complex and multifaceted.  So I feel like when I use Optics Trainer, it's a bit like taking a multivitamin--I'm covering all of my bases--especially when I combine it with classical vision training techniques like Brock String.  And it's super affordable!  

I do Optics Trainer every day.  Thanks for creating Optics Trainer and making it so accessible!  It is a game-changer.

reviews.review_title: Good start but still quite buggy
reviews.review_description:
This game has huge potential but there are still some pretty big issues considering the huge price for the subscription. For example, in the daily training, the bird with the ring doesn't change vergence despite entering the correct answer, it is very hard to reach many of the reaching objects (ice pick/boxing) while playing sitting, the score goes up even when doing nothing in boxing. Hopefully the bugs get sorted then I will subscribe.
Regarding the developer's comment about profile updates, this was another function that never worked for me. It never remembered my previous exercise results. Still lots of bugs to sort.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the feedback. Hopefully you joined our discord where we get all our bug reports, fixes, feature requests. Since your review we added new games, new features, and lowered prices by 50% (beginning Sept 30,2025).

We've released quite a few updates and bugfixes. Let us know what you think!

reviews.review_description: This is the only program of its kind and it is amazing!  The settings automatically adjust based on your personal level and it exercises your ability to use the eyes together.  Love the discord channel to discuss with other people with strabismus and lazy eye.

reviews.review_title: I've tried the Beta and After Beta
reviews.review_description: I've been using Optics trainer since December in it's beta version until 22nd of February as my free trail ended😟. I've used it specially for traning my condition lazy eye and It helped me with my eye's coordination, texture difference and memory, as there's a lot of in-side games that train different aspects. It's a good app and if you're looking to improve your eyesight then I would give it a try, my eye has improved from 6/30 to about 6/15(not only using Optics trainer but it had it's share in it). Would recommend.

Issue resolved fast!! I was able to get a code. Will come back to leave a more detail review of the app. 


I was super excited to try this because I have exotropia and I know how well visual exercise helps me. But it’s asking for a license code. So I couldn’t try it

developer_response.body:
Happy to help! Please contact us at contact@opticstrainer.com to get access to a key!

It should notify you of our contact information immediately after you open the open the the app.

The payment system is not supposed to be active yet, but we're happy to get resolved for you!

Please contact us with any issues and we are happy to fix it.

reviews.review_description: I got this game because I have convergence insufficiency and exophoria. I was told to do exercises to help fix my vision. So far this app has been very helpful. The games are fun and challenging. Some are very hard for me with my vision problems, but I find I am getting better at them. If you have vision issues I recommend this app.
Had some trouble getting into the app at first, but the support was top notch and got me in the right direction quickly
